title: Senior Agentic AI Engineer
notes: Staff/tech-lead build rung bridging agentic-ai-engineer (L30, build) and agentic-systems-architect (L48, design). Owns implementation of production multi-agent subsystems, builds shared eval/observability/reliability infrastructure, leads incident response, and mentors engineers. Build fundamentals are owned by the L30 engineer track and linked, not re-taught; this track adds depth, production scale, and technical leadership. Seeded from role research 2026-06-15; broaden on first nightly research cycle.

modules.title: Implementing Agent Systems from an Architecture
objectives: Turn a reference architecture into a production implementation; own a subsystem end to end
Make and document implementation-level tradeoffs (framework choice, state, tool boundaries)
Refactor a prototype agent into a maintainable, testable codebase
Drive build-vs-buy and complexity decisions at the subsystem level

modules.title: Building Eval & Observability Infrastructure
objectives: Build a reusable evaluation harness (trajectory + tool-call + LLM-judge) for a team
Stand up OpenTelemetry-based tracing and dashboards for agent fleets
Define quality signals and regression gates that block bad deploys
Make eval/observability a paved road other engineers adopt

modules.title: Multi-Agent Systems at Scale
objectives: Implement orchestration topologies that hold up under load and partial failure
Manage token economics and latency budgets in production
Apply durable execution and resumption for long-running agent workloads
Harden inter-agent communication and tool integrations

modules.title: Reliability, Cost & Incident Response
objectives: Define SLOs/SLIs for agentic systems and alert on quality drift, not just uptime
Build cost controls and budgets into running systems
Run incident response for agent failures (bad outputs, runaway loops, tool misuse)
Write postmortems and drive durable fixes

modules.title: Technical Leadership for Agentic Teams
objectives: Lead code review for agent systems with a focus on safety and reliability
Mentor engineers and set implementation standards/paved roads
Translate between the architect's design and the team's execution
Scope and sequence delivery of an agentic initiative

projects.title: Capstone: Lead-Build a Production Multi-Agent Subsystem
projects.description: Implement a production multi-agent subsystem from a given architecture, with a reusable eval harness, OTel tracing, SLOs, cost controls, and durable execution. Deliver an implementation, runbook, and a code-review/standards doc for the team.

projects.title: Reliability & Incident-Response Hardening
projects.description: Take an existing agent system, define SLOs and quality-drift alerts, run a simulated incident (runaway loop / bad output / tool misuse), mitigate it, and write the postmortem with durable fixes.
